label: National Ignition Facility (NIF)
sub: Achieved fusion ignition Dec 5, 2022. Latest record: 5.2 MJ yield (Feb 2024). EYC laser upgrade (2.2→2.6 MJ) cleared CD-1 April 24, 2026. Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, Livermore, CA.

label: ITER — Cadarache, France
sub: 35-nation international tokamak. First plasma now scheduled 2033. Assembly ~30% complete. Budget: €20B+.

label: Commonwealth Fusion Systems — Devens, MA
sub: SPARC ~75% complete, first plasma targeted 2027. First fusion company to apply to PJM grid interconnection (Apr 28, 2026). $3B total raised. MIT spinoff.

label: Helion Energy — Everett, WA
sub: Polaris (7th prototype) operational. $5.4B valuation. Microsoft PPA: 50 MW by 2028. Field-reversed configuration approach.

label: KSTAR — Daejeon, South Korea
sub: World record: 48 seconds at 100M°C (Apr 2024). Targeting 300 seconds by 2026. Korea Institute of Fusion Energy.

label: EAST Tokamak — Hefei, China
sub: World record 1,066-second plasma at 100M°C (Jan 20, 2025). Institute of Plasma Physics, Chinese Academy of Sciences.

label: JT-60SA — Naka, Japan
sub: World's largest operational superconducting tokamak. First plasma Dec 1, 2023. Japan-EU joint project under Broader Approach.

label: Wendelstein 7-X — Greifswald, Germany
sub: World's largest stellarator. Demonstrates steady-state plasma confinement. Max-Planck-Institut für Plasmaphysik.

label: WEST Tokamak — Cadarache, France
sub: World record 1,337-second plasma (Feb 2025). Tests all-tungsten first wall for ITER. CEA / Institut de Recherche sur la Fusion Magnétique.

label: MIT Plasma Science & Fusion Center — Cambridge, MA
sub: Birthplace of CFS. Developed REBCO HTS magnet technology achieving 20 Tesla (Sep 2021). Dennis Whyte, Bob Mumgaard alumni.

label: TAE Technologies — Foothill Ranch, CA
sub: Hydrogen-boron (p-B11) aneutronic fusion. Norman machine. $1.3B+ raised. Google partnership since 2014.

label: General Fusion — Richmond, BC, Canada
sub: Magnetized target fusion (MTF). $492M raised. Targeting public markets via SPAC merger (2026). Backed by Bezos, Temasek.

label: STEP — West Burton A, Nottinghamshire, UK
sub: UK's Spherical Tokamak for Energy Production. £1.3B National Fusion Strategy (Apr 2026); total £2.5B committed (2025–2030). ILIOS Consortium awarded £200M construction partner contract. Operations target: 2040. UKAEA / West Burton A, Nottinghamshire.

label: Culham Centre for Fusion Energy — Abingdon, UK
sub: Home of JET (Joint European Torus, decommissioned 2024). UKAEA headquarters. Previously held the DT fusion energy record (59 MJ in 2022).

label: Max-Planck IPP — Garching, Germany
sub: ASDEX Upgrade tokamak. Theoretical plasma physics research. Partner in Wendelstein 7-X and EUROfusion consortium.

label: CFETR Site — Hefei, China
sub: China Fusion Engineering Test Reactor. Planned completion ~2035. Target: 2 GW peak output. Adjacent to EAST facility.

label: ENN Group Fusion — Langfang, China
sub: China's leading private fusion venture. Hydrogen-boron FRC approach. EXL-50U achieved 1MA plasma current (2024). EHL-2 under construction.

label: Zap Energy — Seattle, WA
sub: Sheared-flow-stabilized Z-pinch approach. Simpler reactor design with no magnets. $160M+ raised. University of Washington spinoff.

label: Marvel Fusion — Munich, Germany
sub: Laser-driven inertial fusion. €113M Series B raised (2024). European private fusion pioneer.

label: NIF Ignition — Dec 5, 2022
sub: Historic ignition shot: 3.15 MJ fusion output from 2.05 MJ laser input. First-ever fusion ignition in a laboratory. Q=1.5 target gain.
